name: Apple Foundation Model 3 Core Advanced (on-device, 20B sparse)
archDisplay: Sparse on-device model (20B total, 1-4B active per request) using Apple's 'Instruction-Following Pruning' with shared + input-routed experts stored in flash and loaded into DRAM on demand
note: Apple's most capable on-device model as of mid-2026, the headline of the WWDC26 third-generation Foundation Models family; natively multimodal (image understanding, expressive voices, higher-accuracy dictation).
verificationNotes:
Confirmed via Apple's own ML research blog post plus independent coverage (9to5Mac, Memeburn, MacRumors, AppleInsider) of the June 8, 2026 WWDC26 keynote/Platforms State of the Union. ctxK=32 carried forward from the prior generation as an estimate (no new figure published in sources found). As with the sibling Core model, only internal human-preference win-rates were published (e.g. >61% preferred on image understanding vs the prior generation) rather than fixed-benchmark scores, so 'results' is left empty rather than guessing a mapping. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No real, citable benchmark results found for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core Advanced (on-device, 20B sparse) after a widened, methodology-diversified search. Confirmed no results exist to report — this is the expected/valid outcome, not a gap in search effort.

What was tried this round (beyond prior English tech-press/HF/arXiv searches already reflected in verificationNotes):
1. Vendor-language check: Apple is a US vendor, not Chinese or Japanese, so Chinese-language sources (36Kr/QbitAI/JiQiZhiXin/SuperCLUE/CLUE/OpenCompass) and Japanese sources (Nejumi/Shaberi/Rakuda) do not apply and were correctly skipped.
2. Artificial Analysis: searched directly (site:artificialanalysis.ai and general queries) — Apple Foundation Models do not appear in AA's leaderboards at all (models are on-device-only, no public API endpoint for AA to benchmark).
3. LMArena / OpenRouter: searched for AFM 3 Core Advanced listings — not found on either; both are chat/API-completion leaderboards and this model has no hosted API surface for third parties to query.
4. Re-fetched Apple's own official launch post (machinelearning.apple.com/research/introducing-third-generation-of-apple-foundation-models) directly and confirmed it contains ONLY human-preference win-rate percentages (e.g., dictation quality 17.6%→44.7%, instruction-following +21%) vs. the 2025 baseline — no MMLU/GSM8K/GPQA/IFEval or any standard benchmark table for any AFM 3 variant.
5. Checked whether Apple's promised "technical report with updated evaluations and benchmarks" (announced as coming "later this summer 2026") has been published yet — as of today (2026-07-15) it has not; independent write-ups (ofox.ai developer read, published post-WWDC) explicitly note "No MMLU, no SWE-bench, no GPQA. Apple's published numbers are preferences against the 2025 baseline only."
6. Found and ruled out a false-positive: an AppleLeaker X post and aggregator sites (gpt0x.com) cite MMLU 67.85% / MGSM 74.91% — but on closer reading these figures belong to a DIFFERENT, smaller model, "AFM 3 Core" (a ~3B dense on-device model), not "AFM 3 Core Advanced" (the 20B sparse model this corpus entry is about). Conflating the two would be exactly the kind of variant-mixup error flagged as the corpus's top known risk (see dataset-audit-2026-07 memory), so this was deliberately NOT applied to this entry.
7. Checked gpt0x.com's benchmark tracker page directly by fetch — no entry for AFM 3 Core Advanced (or any Apple on-device 20B model) exists there.

Conclusion: this remains a genuine "no public benchmark data exists" case. Apple has, as of publication, only released internal human-preference comparisons for this specific model, not fixed-benchmark scores compatible with the RankedModel taxonomy. Recommend re-checking once Apple's promised summer-2026 technical report is published (watch machinelearning.apple.com/research/), but do not force-fit the AFM 3 Core (3B) benchmark numbers onto this 20B sparse Core Advanced entry — they are a different model variant.

ROUND-8 STRUCTURED-DATA-FEED CHECK (2026-07-15): Checked the two specific structured sources not previously exhausted. (1) Epoch AI raw data feed: downloaded both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, benchmark-definition metadata only) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, the actual per-model-per-benchmark results feed) directly via curl and grepped case-insensitively for 'apple', 'afm', 'foundation model', 'on-device' in any form — zero matching model rows (the only 'apple' hits were unrelated surnames 'Applebaum'/'appler'; 'foundation model' appears once as a generic phrase, not tied to any Apple row). Epoch AI does not track any Apple Foundation Model variant. (2) Vals.ai: fetched https://www.vals.ai/models — full provider list includes OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, DeepSeek, xAI/SpaceXAI, etc. but no Apple entry at all; tried a direct model-page guess at https://www.vals.ai/models/apple_afm-3-core-advanced per the vendor_model-slug pattern — returned HTTP 404. Vals.ai does not benchmark this model (consistent with it having no public API endpoint for a third party to run standard evals against). Also checked as a bonus: paperswithcode.com search for 'Apple Foundation Model' now 302-redirects to huggingface.co/papers/trending (the standalone PWC site/search no longer functions independently) — no dedicated leaderboard page found either way. HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard was not separately re-checked this round since Apple's on-device models are closed-weight and have never been submittable to that leaderboard (established in prior rounds). Final conclusion unchanged: no real, citable benchmark result exists for this model from any source checked across all 8 research rounds. This is confirmed as the correct, final state absent a new Apple publication. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): No benchmark results found for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core Advanced (on-device, 20B sparse) after checking the two specific structured sources requested, plus two bonus checks. All four came back negative:

1. Epoch AI raw data feed: downloaded both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, benchmark-definition metadata) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, the actual per-model results feed) via curl and grepped case-insensitively for "apple", "afm", "foundation model", "on-device" in any form. Zero matching model rows — the only "apple" hits were unrelated surnames (Applebaum/appler); "foundation model" appears once as a generic phrase unconnected to any Apple row. Epoch AI does not track any Apple Foundation Model variant at all.
